settings.languageDescription: Choose the language used by the editor interface.
settings.title: Settings

saveLoad.invalidJson: File could not be parsed as JSON.

loadDialog.noRecognisedNodes: The file contains no recognised nodes.

audioDialog.title: Audio settings
audioDialog.description: Adjust volume levels and mute settings.

keyboardDialog.title: Keyboard shortcuts
keyboardDialog.description: Shortcuts are context-aware. Guided constraints are enabled by default; hold Shift during an active gesture to build freely.

5.action: Select next level in the active building

6.action: Select previous level in the active building

4.action: Cancel the active tool and return to Select mode

0.action: Add or remove an object from multi-selection
0.note: Works in Select mode on the 3D canvas, the 2D floor plan, and the scene graph.

1.action: Add or remove an object from canvas multi-selection
1.note: In the scene graph, Shift-click selects the visible range like a file browser.

2.note: With 2+ objects selected, in 2D and 3D alike: drag the selection (or its dashed box) to slide it; click it to pick it up and place with the next click.

3.action: Rotate a multi-selection ±45° around its center
3.note: Also works mid-move while carrying the selection.

4.note: Clicking empty space does the same.

0.action: Move the selected movable object under the cursor
0.note: Drag in Select mode with a single object selected. Guided snapping and guides are enabled by default.

1.action: Rotate the selected object under the cursor
1.note: Drag left or right in Select mode with a single object selected. Rotation snaps to 15° increments by default.

2.note: Hold Shift during the drag to bypass the 15° rotation increment.

0.action: Bypass guided snapping and angle constraints
0.note: Hold during the active gesture. Passive guide or measurement feedback may stay visible.

1.action: Rotate freely, bypassing the default 15° rotation snap
1.note: Hold while dragging a rotate handle or direct-rotation gesture.

0.action: Rotate item; with a door selected, R toggles open/closed and T closes

1.action: Operate the selected node — doors, windows, and cabinet doors/drawers animate open/closed

0.note: Moves in screen space, similar to dragging the camera view.

1.note: Drag with the middle mouse button, or hold Space while dragging with the left mouse button.

2.note: Drag with the right mouse button.

nature.title: Nature

instructions.arming: Click the ground to plant. Press Esc to stop.
instructions.trees: Pick a tree, then click the ground.
instructions.flowers: Pick a flower, then click the ground.
instructions.grass: Pick grass, then click the ground.

nature.description: Procedural trees, flowers, and grasses for outdoor scenes.

kindDescriptions.tree: A procedural tree. Oak, pine, aspen, ash, bush, or trellis.
kindDescriptions.flower: A procedural flower. Daisy, tulip, or lavender.
kindDescriptions.grass: A procedural grass tuft. Meadow, fescue, or reed.

cabinet.tooltip: Placement type — click or press I to toggle

legacyUiText.Add focused tools and content to this pr: Add focused tools and content to this project.
Adds a new tool panel to the editor.
legacyUiText.Creator: Creator
legacyUiText.Drag the area you want to capture: Drag the area you want to capture
legacyUiText.Enter the real-world length of the line: Enter the real-world length of the line you just drew. The image will resize to match it.
legacyUiText.No collections yet. Create one to group: No collections yet. Create one to group items together.
legacyUiText.No custom materials yet — add one with +: No custom materials yet — add one with +.
legacyUiText.No drain, waste, or vent pipes yet. Draw: No drain, waste, or vent pipes yet. Draw plumbing to see the riser diagram.
legacyUiText.Overlay image unavailable. Replace the i: Overlay image unavailable. Replace the image to restore it.
legacyUiText.Select a level to view and create zones: Select a level to view and create zones
legacyUiText.Switch to a building level to view and e: Switch to a building level to view and edit the floorplan.
legacyUiText.freezes this exact camera angle as a reu: freezes this exact camera angle as a reusable reference for renders & videos.
legacyUiText.A snapshot freezes this exact camera ang: A snapshot freezes this exact camera angle as a reusable reference for renders & videos.
legacyUiText.This panel hit an error and was unloaded: This panel hit an error and was unloaded for this session. The rest of the editor is unaffected — reload to try again.
legacyUiText.Flat and fast — no ambient occlusion: Flat and fast — no ambient occlusion
legacyUiText.Any decimal works. Use the known real le: Any decimal works. Use the known real length, not the drawn value.
legacyUiText.Click both ends of a known distance on t: Click both ends of a known distance on the plan, then type its real length.
legacyUiText.Click one end of a distance you know — e: Click one end of a distance you know — e.g. a dimension printed on the plan
legacyUiText.Click the other end of that distance: Click the other end of that distance
legacyUiText.Draw a line over a known dimension on th: Draw a line over a known dimension on the plan, then type its real length to scale the image exactly.
legacyUiText.selected elements? This cannot be undone: selected elements? This cannot be undone if the undo history is exhausted.
Enter the real length of the line.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metadataDescription: Privacy Policy for Pascal Editor and the Pascal platform.

paragraphs: Pascal Group Inc. ("we," "us," or "our") operates the Pascal Editor and Platform at pascal.app. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when you use our services.

blocks.heading: Account Information
paragraphs: When you create an account, we collect:
items: Email address
Name
Profile picture/avatar
OAuth provider data (from Google when you sign in with Google)

blocks.heading: Project Data
paragraphs: When you use the Platform, we store your projects, including 3D building designs, floor plans, and associated metadata.

blocks.heading: Usage Analytics
paragraphs: We use Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights to collect anonymized usage data, including page views, performance metrics, and general usage patterns. This helps us improve the Platform.
We use your information to:
items: Provide and maintain your account
Store and sync your projects across devices
Improve our services based on usage patterns
Send optional email notifications about new features and updates (you can opt out in settings)
Respond to support requests
Ensure platform security and prevent abuse

paragraphs: Your data is stored using Supabase (PostgreSQL database) on secure cloud infrastructure. We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data.
We use the following third-party services to operate the Platform:
items: Google - OAuth authentication for sign-in
Vercel - Application hosting, analytics, and performance monitoring
Supabase - Database hosting and authentication infrastructure

paragraphs: Each of these services has their own privacy policies governing their handling of your data.
We use minimal cookies necessary for the Platform to function:
items: Session cookies - Essential for authentication and keeping you signed in
Analytics cookies - Used by Vercel Analytics to collect anonymized usage data

paragraphs: You have the right to:
items: Access the personal data we hold about you
Request correction of inaccurate data
Request deletion of your data
Export your project data
Opt out of marketing communications
blocks.contactPrefix: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at

paragraphs: We retain your data for as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, we will delete your personal data and project data within 30 days, except where we are required by law to retain certain information.

The Platform is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe we have collected such information, please contact us immediately.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated policy on the Platform. Your continued use of the Platform after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the revised policy.

blocks.contactPrefix: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please contact us at

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.metadataDescription: Terms of Service for Pascal Editor and the Pascal platform.

paragraphs: Welcome to Pascal Editor ("Editor") and the Pascal platform at pascal.app ("Platform"), operated by Pascal Group Inc. ("we," "us," or "our"). By accessing or using our services, you agree to these Terms of Service.

The Pascal Editor is open-source software released under the MIT License. You may use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Editor software in accordance with the MIT License terms.

The Pascal platform (pascal.app) and its associated services, including user accounts, cloud storage, and project hosting, are proprietary services owned and operated by Pascal Group Inc. These Terms govern your use of the Platform.

To use certain features of the Platform, you must create an account. We use Google OAuth and magic link email authentication through Supabase. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.
You agree not to:
items: Use the Platform for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any applicable laws
Upload, share, or distribute content that infringes intellectual property rights
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Platform or its systems
Interfere with or disrupt the Platform's infrastructure
Upload malicious code, viruses, or harmful content
Harass, abuse, or harm other users
Use the Platform to send spam or unsolicited communications

paragraphs: You retain full ownership of all content, projects, and data you create or upload to the Platform ("Your Content"). By using the Platform, you grant us a limited license to store, display, and transmit Your Content solely to provide our services to you.
We do not claim any ownership rights over Your Content. You may export or delete Your Content at any time.

The Platform, including its design, features, and proprietary code, is owned by Pascal Group Inc. and protected by intellectual property laws. While the Editor source code is open-source under the MIT License, the Platform services, branding, and infrastructure remain our proprietary property.

blocks.contactPrefix: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms or engage in conduct that we determine is harmful to the Platform or other users. You may also delete your account at any time by contacting us at

paragraphs: THE PLATFORM IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.
We do not warrant that the Platform will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of harmful components.

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, PASCAL GROUP INC. SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF DATA, PROFITS, OR GOODWILL, ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE PLATFORM.

We may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated Terms on the Platform. Your continued use of the Platform after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

blocks.contactPrefix: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us at
